concerns.text: T014a instructs to 'downscale (to high-resolution)' which semantically inverts FR-002 and US-1. The spec requires downscaling to 'coarse spatial resolution' (30m/pixel) to simulate degradation. A task that generates higher resolution data than the input violates the core constraint of the degradation pipeline.

T012 implements a loop to download 500 1km² tiles but lacks the explicit step to extract 100m² patches required by FR-003 and SC-003 before degradation/generation. The spec mandates generation on 100m² patches to fit CPU constraints; downloading 1km² tiles without a defined extraction task creates a scope mismatch and risks violating the compute budget (Constitution Principle VII).

T026 aggregates batch runtimes but fails to mandate per-sample logging of `peak_ram_mb` and `wall_clock_time` required by SC-003. The spec requires measuring feasibility 'per scene' to verify the 6.5GB/45min constraints. Aggregating without per-sample granularity prevents the verification of individual sample compliance, weakening the acceptance criterion.

T032 mentions sweeping NNF thresholds but omits the explicit requirement to calculate the p-value at each step to identify the critical threshold where p > 0.05. SC-002 and FR-005 define the threshold specifically by this statistical significance boundary. Without the p-value calculation step, the task cannot satisfy the acceptance criterion for identifying the 'critical failure point'.

T011b and T016 reference tuning parameters against a 'real cloud masks subset' but do not explicitly define the statistical comparison method (e.g., distribution similarity test) required by FR-006. FR-006 mandates 'validating... by comparing statistical properties'. Tuning parameters without a defined validation metric risks a silent relaxation of the verification requirement.
